constraints: All existing functionality must be preserved — this is a refactor, not a feature change
Changes must be incremental and testable — no big-bang rewrite
20 existing test files in video-editor must continue passing (1427 lines of test code in hooks/lib alone)
Context consumers (80+ properties on TimelineEditorContext) must not break — components throughout the editor depend on these
Save/conflict-retry/poll logic must remain correct — data loss or version conflicts are critical bugs
Remotion composition pipeline (TimelineRenderer, AudioTrack, Player) must not regress — remount semantics matter
Undo/redo (useTimelineHistory) depends on commitData and onBeforeCommit ref threading — must not break
React Query polling (30s interval via useTimelineQueries) integration must remain intact
Upload-in-progress preservation (preserveUploadingClips) must work across all edit and poll paths
Audio key={clip.id-clip.at} remount fix must be preserved and ideally made more principled

key_evidence.point: useTimelineData returns 80+ properties by manually threading results from 6 sub-hooks (save, history, derived, render, assets, prefs). It's a pure pass-through layer with no logic except wiring onBeforeCommitRef.

applyTimelineEdit (line 451) works on rows+metaUpdates and calls materializeData→commitData. applyResolvedConfigEdit (line 494) takes a full resolvedConfig and calls buildDataFromCurrentRegistry→commitData. Different materialization paths, different semantics for meta handling.

getPollRejectionReason checks 4 conditions sequentially (unsaved edits, pending ops, stale version, signature match). pendingOpsRef must be manually incremented/decremented by every async operation. Missing a decrement = polls blocked forever.

useDerivedTimeline signature caching (lines 22-35): compares data.signature to a ref, only updates stableResolvedConfigRef when signature changes. Purpose is preventing Remotion remounts on poll churn, but creates a window where resolvedConfig doesn't match data.

resolveGroupOverlaps (multi-clip) returns only rows, not metaPatches. Line 248 has explicit TODO: 'applyMultiDragMoves cannot propagate matching from/to meta patches yet'. This means multi-clip drag of timed clips silently loses trim metadata.
AudioTrack uses key={clip.id}-${clip.at} to force remount on position change. This is a workaround — Web Audio API buffers don't respond to React prop changes the way DOM elements do.
VideoEditorProvider.tsx nests three context providers (Editor > Chrome > Playback) and calls useTimelineState() which orchestrates everything. This is the single entry point.

commitData (useTimelineSave.ts ~line 402) is the single funnel for all state updates. It handles: setting data state, updating dataRef, pruning selection, incrementing editSeq, scheduling saves, and calling onBeforeCommit for history snapshots.

useTimelineState.ts imports 11 hooks and composes their results into editor/chrome/playback context values. The useClipDrag hook is conspicuously NOT imported — it's consumed via useDragCoordinator or passed differently.

Test coverage exists for: track management (122 lines), history/undo (359 lines), clip editing (128 lines), clip drag (214 lines), external drop (226 lines), save utils (319 lines), serialize (59 lines). No tests for useDerivedTimeline, useTimelineData, or useTimelineState composition.

relevant_code.why: 738-line mega-hook containing save queue, poll sync, conflict retry, data materialization, and two divergent edit paths. Primary decomposition target.
165-line pass-through layer composing 6 hooks and returning 80+ properties. Candidate for elimination or significant thinning.
436-line orchestrator composing 11+ hooks into three context values. Top of the hook hierarchy — restructuring flows down from here.
Defines the 80+ property context interface. Must be updated as context shape changes.
105-line hook with signature-based caching that creates data/resolvedConfig divergence window. Needs evaluation for removal or replacement.
resolveGroupOverlaps has known TODO — doesn't produce metaPatches for multi-clip drag. resolveOverlaps silently adjusts positions without feedback.
Consumer of resolveGroupOverlaps. applyMultiDragMoves cannot propagate meta patches due to resolveGroupOverlaps limitation.
487 lines. Manages drag sessions, pendingOpsRef increment/decrement, calls applyTimelineEdit. Key consumer of the edit path.
539 lines. Major consumer of applyTimelineEdit and resolveOverlaps. Will be affected by edit path unification.
469 lines. moveClipToRow uses applyTimelineEdit, createTrackAndMoveClip uses applyResolvedConfigEdit — demonstrates the dual-path problem.
790 lines, largest hook. Multiple calls to applyTimelineEdit and resolveOverlaps. Will be affected by edit path changes.
Uses key={clip.id-clip.at} workaround for Web Audio remount. Needs principled audio lifecycle approach.
Root Remotion composition — renders visual and audio tracks. Audio handling changes flow through here.
Mounts the three context providers. Entry point where useTimelineState result is distributed.
343 lines. Depends on commitData and onBeforeCommit ref threading. Must be preserved through save decomposition.
Core data transformation functions used by both edit paths. rowsToConfig is the materialization step for the rows-based path.
Used by applyResolvedConfigEdit path and poll acceptance logic. Has 319 lines of tests.

suggested_approach:
Execute as 7 incremental phases, each independently testable:

**Phase 1: Decompose useTimelineSave (highest value, most isolated)**
Split into 3 focused modules:
- `useTimelinePersistence` — save mutation, debounce/queue, conflict retry (the IO boundary)
- `usePollSync` — poll acceptance/rejection, signature tracking. Invert to default-reject: replace pendingOpsRef counter with an `isIdle` derivation (idle = savedSeq >= editSeq AND no in-flight save AND no active drag/resize). Callers no longer need to remember increment/decrement.
- `useTimelineCommit` — commitData, applyEdit (unified), materializeData, state management. This becomes the single edit entry point.

**Phase 2: Unify edit paths**
Replace applyTimelineEdit + applyResolvedConfigEdit with a single `applyEdit(mutation)` that accepts either rows+meta or resolvedConfig and normalizes internally via materializeData/buildDataFromCurrentRegistry. All 6+ call sites (useClipDrag, useClipEditing, useClipResize, useExternalDrop, useTimelineTrackManagement, useAssetManagement) update to the unified API. createTrackAndMoveClip must go through overlap resolution like everything else.

**Phase 3: Flatten useTimelineData**
useTimelineData is a pass-through. Eliminate it by having useTimelineState call the sub-hooks directly (save persistence, poll sync, commit, history, derived, render, assets, prefs). This removes one composition layer and makes the dependency graph shallower.

**Phase 4: Make resolveOverlaps return feedback**
- resolveOverlaps returns `{ rows, metaPatches, adjustments: Array<{clipId, requestedStart, actualStart}> }` 
- resolveGroupOverlaps returns `{ rows, metaPatches }` (fix the TODO — produce meta patches for timed clips)
- Callers can surface adjustments to UI (toast, visual indicator) — but that's a feature addition for later

**Phase 5: Evaluate useDerivedTimeline signature caching**
Profile whether removing the signature cache actually causes Remotion remount churn. If polling is already rejected during edits (Phase 1 fix), the cache may be unnecessary. If still needed, make it explicit: `useStableRef(resolvedConfig, data.signature)` utility rather than inline ref juggling.

**Phase 6: Audio lifecycle**
Create an `AudioClipSequence` component that wraps the Remotion Sequence+Audio pair with proper lifecycle:
- Uses `useEffect` cleanup to properly dispose Web Audio buffers
- Handles remount on position/trim changes explicitly rather than via key hack
- TimelineRenderer delegates audio tracks to this component

**Phase 7: Thin the contexts**
Split TimelineEditorContext (80+ properties) into sub-contexts by concern:
- `TimelineDataContext` — data, resolvedConfig, dataRef, isLoading
- `TimelineSelectionContext` — selectedClipId, selectedTrackId, multi-select operations  
- `TimelineEditContext` — applyEdit, clip editing operations, track management
- `TimelineInteractionContext` — drag, resize, external drop handlers
Keep the existing provider nesting in VideoEditorProvider but with more focused interfaces.

**Testing strategy**: Run existing tests after each phase. Add integration tests for the unified edit path. The 319-line timeline-save-utils.test.ts is the critical regression suite for Phases 1-2.
task_summary: Restructure the video editor's timeline state management by decomposing the 738-line useTimelineSave into focused concerns, unifying two divergent edit paths (applyTimelineEdit vs applyResolvedConfigEdit), inverting poll rejection to default-reject, flattening the 3-layer hook hierarchy, fixing resolveGroupOverlaps' missing meta patches, and giving audio clips proper lifecycle handling. This is a pure refactor across ~16 hook files and ~4,800 lines of code.

test_expectations.what_it_checks: Track CRUD operations, clip movement between tracks, track reordering
Undo/redo stack, checkpoint creation/restoration, commit history integration
Clip updates, deletion, splitting, muting operations via applyTimelineEdit
Drag session lifecycle, position updates, pendingOpsRef management, overlap resolution
External asset drag-over/drop handling, asset registration, timeline insertion
shouldAcceptPolledData logic, buildDataFromCurrentRegistry, save data serialization
Render boundary calculation from timeline config
Validation of render bounds including rowsToConfig integration
